questions.prompt:
A researcher studies whether hydration affects short-term attention. Group A drinks 500 mL of water one hour before a computer attention test. Group B drinks no water during that hour. Both groups eat the same breakfast and take the same test in the same room.
Group | Average correct responses out of 50
A | 43
B | 36
Which conclusion is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Drinking water before the test was associated with more correct responses.
choices.B: Drinking water before the test was associated with fewer correct responses.
choices.C: Hydration had no possible relationship to attention.
choices.D: Both groups had identical attention-test results.

questions.correctAnswerText: Drinking water before the test was associated with more correct responses.
questions.explanation: Group A drank water and had a higher average score than Group B.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Torque can be calculated as force × distance from the pivot.
Lever | Force (N) | Distance from pivot (m)
A | 20 | 0.5
B | 15 | 1.0
C | 12 | 1.5
D | 8 | 2.0
Which lever setup produces the greatest torque?

choices.A: Lever A
choices.B: Lever B
choices.D: Lever D

questions.correctAnswerText: Lever C
questions.explanation: Torque = force × distance. Lever C produces 12 N × 1.5 m = 18 N·m, the greatest value listed.

questions.prompt:
Lithium is used in many rechargeable batteries. Mining lithium can provide material for energy-storage technology, but mining may also use large amounts of water and disturb habitats. Recycling batteries can recover some lithium and other metals.
Which statement best describes one environmental benefit of battery recycling?

choices.A: It can reduce the need to mine new raw materials.
choices.B: It creates unlimited lithium from air.
choices.C: It stops all use of batteries immediately.
choices.D: It changes lithium into a renewable food source.

questions.correctAnswerText: It can reduce the need to mine new raw materials.
questions.explanation: Recycling can recover useful metals and reduce demand for new mining.

questions.prompt:
Blood calcium is controlled partly by two hormones. Parathyroid hormone can raise blood calcium when levels are too low. Calcitonin can help lower blood calcium when levels are too high. These responses help keep calcium within a narrow range needed for nerve and muscle function.
Which idea is best illustrated by this process?

choices.A: Hormonal feedback helps maintain homeostasis.
choices.B: Calcium levels never change in the blood.
choices.C: Hormones prevent nerves and muscles from working.
choices.D: Blood calcium is controlled only by the lungs.

questions.correctAnswerText: Hormonal feedback helps maintain homeostasis.
questions.explanation: Hormones that raise or lower blood calcium help keep internal conditions stable.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A person pushes down on a wrench with a force of 90 N. The force is applied 0.5 m from the bolt.
Torque = force × distance from pivot
What torque is produced in N·m?
Type your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Torque = 90 N × 0.5 m = 45 N·m.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A student tests how axle position affects how far a small cart rolls after a push. The same cart body and the same starting push are used in every trial. Only the axle position is changed.
Which TWO factors should be kept constant?

choices.A: Cart body
choices.B: Starting push
choices.D: Distance rolled

questions.correctAnswerText: Cart body | Starting push
questions.explanation: The cart body and starting push should stay the same so axle position is the tested variable.

questions.prompt: Some traits are influenced by gene expression, which means whether a gene is actively used to make a product. Environmental factors can sometimes affect gene expression without changing the DNA sequence. This kind of regulation affects [D1] and does not necessarily change [D2].

D1: how strongly a gene is expressed

questions.correct: D1=how strongly a gene is expressed; D2=the DNA sequence itself
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=how strongly a gene is expressed; D2=the DNA sequence itself
questions.explanation: Environmental factors may influence gene expression without changing the DNA sequence.

questions.prompt:
A student carefully combines a weak acid and a weak base in a beaker. The temperature rises slightly, and the final solution is closer to neutral than either starting solution. The student identifies the process as neutralization.
Which statement best describes neutralization?

choices.A: An acid and a base react to form a more neutral solution.
choices.B: A solid changes directly into a gas without energy transfer.
choices.C: A magnet separates iron from sand.
choices.D: A chromosome copies itself before cell division.

questions.correctAnswerText: An acid and a base react to form a more neutral solution.
questions.explanation: Neutralization occurs when an acid and a base react, producing a solution closer to neutral.

questions.prompt:
Astronomers observe that light from a distant galaxy is shifted toward longer, redder wavelengths. This redshift is evidence that the galaxy is moving away from Earth. Observations of many galaxies show a similar pattern.
Which conclusion is best supported by this evidence?

choices.A: Many distant galaxies are moving away from Earth.
choices.B: Galaxies are made only of liquid water.
choices.C: Redshift means stars have stopped producing light.
choices.D: Earth is the only object in the universe that moves.

questions.correctAnswerText: Many distant galaxies are moving away from Earth.
questions.explanation: Redshift toward longer wavelengths is evidence that distant galaxies are moving away.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A doctor measures kidney filtration rate in four patients.
Patient | Estimated filtration rate (mL/min)
A | 96
B | 72
C | 48
D | 88
A lower filtration rate may indicate reduced kidney function.
Which patient has the lowest estimated filtration rate?

choices.A: Patient A
choices.B: Patient B
choices.D: Patient D

questions.correctAnswerText: Patient C
questions.explanation: Patient C has the lowest listed filtration rate, 48 mL/min.

questions.prompt: Match each target with the correct tile.

tiles.A: Number of gear teeth on the driver gear
tiles.B: Rotation speed of the output gear
tiles.C: Same driven gear used in every trial

questions.correctAnswerText: T1=Number of gear teeth on the driver gear; T2=Rotation speed of the output gear; T3=Same driven gear used in every trial
questions.explanation: The driver gear teeth are changed, output rotation speed is measured, and the driven gear is controlled.

questions.prompt:
Bone marrow contains stem cells that can produce different types of blood cells. Some cells develop into red blood cells that carry oxygen. Others develop into white blood cells involved in immune defense or platelets involved in clotting.
Which statement best describes one function of bone marrow?

choices.A: It produces cells that become blood cells.
choices.B: It bends light through a glass lens.
choices.D: It forms lava from cooled magma.

questions.correctAnswerText: It produces cells that become blood cells.
questions.explanation: Bone marrow contains stem cells that produce blood cell types.

questions.prompt:
Two sound waves meet in the same space. In one case, the high points of the waves line up and the sound becomes louder. In another case, a high point lines up with a low point and the sound becomes quieter.
Which wave behavior is described?

choices.A: Interference
choices.B: Digestion
choices.D: Plate collision

questions.correctAnswerText: Interference
questions.explanation: Waves can combine to make a sound louder or quieter depending on how their high and low points line up.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A city reduces annual coal use from 50,000 tons to 38,000 tons after switching part of its energy supply to renewable sources.
What is the percent decrease in coal use?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: Coal use decreased by 12,000 tons. 12,000 ÷ 50,000 × 100 = 24%.

questions.prompt:
A researcher tests whether a new meal plan affects cholesterol level. One group follows the meal plan for 10 weeks. A comparison group continues its usual diet. Both groups have cholesterol measured at the beginning and end of the study.
Why should cholesterol be measured before and after the study?

choices.A: To compare changes in cholesterol over time for each group
choices.B: To make cholesterol impossible to detect
choices.C: To guarantee both groups have the same final result
choices.D: To avoid using any data from the comparison group

questions.correctAnswerText: To compare changes in cholesterol over time for each group
questions.explanation: Measuring before and after allows researchers to compare how cholesterol changed.

questions.prompt:
A student compares how different blade pitches affect the lift produced by a small fan blade. The same motor and same blade material are used for every trial. A sensor measures lift force.
What is the dependent variable?

choices.A: Lift force measured by the sensor
choices.B: Blade pitch
choices.D: Same blade material

questions.correctAnswerText: Lift force measured by the sensor
questions.explanation: The dependent variable is the measured outcome after changing blade pitch.

questions.prompt: A fixed pulley changes the direction of an input force. A movable pulley can reduce the input force needed to lift a load. In this description, the fixed pulley mainly changes [D1], while the movable pulley can provide [D2].
questions.correctAnswerText: D1=force direction; D2=mechanical advantage
questions.explanation: A fixed pulley changes force direction, while a movable pulley can reduce input force.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A trainer measures muscle fatigue by recording how long participants can hold a light weight with the arm extended.
Participant | Hold time (seconds)
A | 42
B | 67
C | 51
D | 74
Which participant held the weight the longest?

choices.A: Participant A
choices.B: Participant B
choices.D: Participant D

questions.correctAnswerText: Participant D
questions.explanation: Participant D held the weight the longest, 74 seconds.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares the output of different gear pairs.
Gear pair | Input rotations | Output rotations
A | 10 | 20
B | 10 | 15
C | 10 | 10
D | 10 | 5
Which gear pair produces the greatest increase in rotation number?

choices.A: Gear pair A
choices.B: Gear pair B
choices.D: Gear pair D

questions.correctAnswerText: Gear pair A
questions.explanation: Gear pair A produces 20 output rotations from 10 input rotations, the greatest increase.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A coastal region tracks mangrove coverage and estimated storm-damage cost.
Year | Mangrove coverage (hectares) | Estimated storm-damage cost (million $)
1 | 1,200 | 86
2 | 1,450 | 74
3 | 1,700 | 63
4 | 1,950 | 55
Which TWO statements are best supported by the data?

choices.A: Mangrove coverage increased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.B: Estimated storm-damage cost decreased from Year 1 to Year 4.
choices.D: Storm-damage cost was highest in Year 4.
choices.E: Both measurements stayed exactly the same.

questions.correctAnswerText: Mangrove coverage increased from Year 1 to Year 4. | Estimated storm-damage cost decreased from Year 1 to Year 4.
questions.explanation: Mangrove coverage increased from 1,200 to 1,950 hectares, while estimated damage cost decreased from $86 million to $55 million.

questions.prompt:
During meiosis, chromosome pairs separate so reproductive cells receive one chromosome from each pair. If chromosomes fail to separate correctly, a reproductive cell may receive too many or too few chromosomes.
Which result can be caused by this kind of error?

choices.A: An abnormal chromosome number in an offspring
choices.B: A change in mineral hardness
choices.C: A decrease in the Moon’s orbital period
choices.D: Formation of igneous rock from lava

questions.correctAnswerText: An abnormal chromosome number in an offspring
questions.explanation: Errors in chromosome separation can produce reproductive cells with too many or too few chromosomes.

questions.prompt:
Dry ice is solid carbon dioxide. At room temperature, dry ice changes directly from a solid to a gas without becoming liquid first.
Which phase change is described?

choices.A: Sublimation
choices.B: Freezing
choices.D: Cementation

questions.correctAnswerText: Sublimation
questions.explanation: Sublimation is the change from solid directly to gas.

questions.prompt:
A metamorphic rock forms near a magma intrusion. The original rock does not melt, but heat from the nearby magma changes its minerals and texture. Pressure is not the main factor in this case.
Which process is described?

choices.A: Contact metamorphism
choices.B: River deposition
choices.D: Wind transport only

questions.correctAnswerText: Contact metamorphism
questions.explanation: Contact metamorphism occurs when heat from nearby magma changes rock without melting it.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A hospital reviews 420 patient records. Of these records, 63 show a documented medication allergy.
What percent of the records show a documented medication allergy?
Type your answer as a percent rounded to the nearest whole number.

A student tests how the thickness of protective padding affects peak impact force. The same mass is dropped from the same height onto padding samples of different thicknesses. A force sensor records the peak impact force.
Which factor is the independent variable?

choices.A: Padding thickness
choices.B: Peak impact force
choices.D: Drop height

questions.correctAnswerText: Padding thickness
questions.explanation: The independent variable is the factor intentionally changed by the student.

questions.prompt:
Peroxisomes are organelles that contain enzymes used to break down certain fatty acids and detoxify harmful substances such as hydrogen peroxide. These reactions help protect the cell from chemical damage.
Which statement best describes peroxisomes?

choices.A: They help break down harmful substances inside cells.
choices.B: They move tectonic plates under continents.
choices.D: They carry oxygen through arteries.

questions.correctAnswerText: They help break down harmful substances inside cells.
questions.explanation: Peroxisomes contain enzymes that detoxify certain harmful substances.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student records the height reached by the same ball after it is launched with different stored spring energies.
Stored spring energy (J): 2, 4, 6, 8
Maximum height (m): 0.4, 0.8, 1.2, 1.6
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Greater stored spring energy was associated with greater maximum height.
choices.B: Greater stored spring energy was associated with lower maximum height.
choices.C: Maximum height stayed the same for every energy value.
choices.D: Stored spring energy had no relationship to ball height.

questions.correctAnswerText: Greater stored spring energy was associated with greater maximum height.
questions.explanation: Maximum height increased from 0.4 m to 1.6 m as stored spring energy increased.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student compares the rotation periods of four planets.
Planet | Rotation period (Earth hours)
A | 10
B | 24
C | 58
D | 243
Which planet has the shortest rotation period?

choices.A: Planet A
choices.B: Planet B
choices.D: Planet D

questions.correctAnswerText: Planet A
questions.explanation: Planet A has the shortest rotation period, 10 Earth hours.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A doctor records blood glucose level after a patient receives insulin.
Time after insulin (min): 0, 30, 60, 90
Blood glucose (mg/dL): 190, 154, 118, 96
Which statement is best supported by the data?

choices.A: Blood glucose decreased after insulin was given.
choices.B: Blood glucose increased after insulin was given.
choices.C: Blood glucose stayed exactly the same.
choices.D: Insulin made blood glucose disappear instantly.

questions.correctAnswerText: Blood glucose decreased after insulin was given.
questions.explanation: Blood glucose decreased from 190 mg/dL to 96 mg/dL over 90 minutes.

questions.prompt:
A student places a warm metal block on a cooler ceramic tile. After several minutes, the metal block cools and the ceramic tile warms.
Which statement best describes the energy transfer?

choices.A: Thermal energy transfers from the warmer metal block to the cooler tile.
choices.B: Cold energy moves from the tile into the metal block.
choices.C: Thermal energy cannot move between solids.
choices.D: The tile produces gravity to cool the metal.

questions.correctAnswerText: Thermal energy transfers from the warmer metal block to the cooler tile.
questions.explanation: Thermal energy naturally moves from a warmer object to a cooler object.

questions.prompt:
Select TWO answers.
A researcher studies whether a new coordination exercise improves balance in people recovering from a mild nerve injury. One group completes the exercise program, and another group receives usual care. Balance is measured after six weeks.
Which TWO choices would improve the investigation?

choices.A: Use the same balance-measurement method for both groups.
choices.B: Include enough participants in both groups.
choices.C: Record only the results from the exercise group.
choices.D: Give one group a longer testing period.
choices.E: Change the balance test for each participant.

questions.correctAnswerText: Use the same balance-measurement method for both groups. | Include enough participants in both groups.
questions.explanation: Consistent measurement and adequate sample size improve reliability.

questions.skill: Using Numbers and Graphics in Science
questions.prompt:
A student measures the time for different objects to complete one full vibration.
Object | Time for 1 vibration (s)
A | 0.25
B | 0.50
C | 1.00
D | 2.00
Frequency = 1 ÷ period
Which object has the highest frequency?

choices.A: Object A
choices.B: Object B
choices.D: Object D

questions.correctAnswerText: Object A
questions.explanation: Frequency = 1 ÷ period. Object A has the shortest period, so it has the highest frequency.

questions.prompt:
After a major flood, a wetland area remains covered with nutrient-rich sediment. In the following years, fast-growing plants spread first, followed by shrubs and young trees as the habitat changes.
Which ecological process is described?

choices.A: Secondary succession
choices.B: Electrical induction
choices.D: Acid-base neutralization

questions.correctAnswerText: Secondary succession
questions.explanation: Secondary succession can occur after a disturbance when soil or sediment remains.

questions.prompt:
A person inherits a changed copy of a gene involved in blood clotting. The change can reduce the amount of working clotting protein, making bleeding last longer after an injury.
Which statement best explains the effect of the gene change?

choices.A: A gene change can affect a protein involved in blood clotting.
choices.B: A gene change always improves clotting.
choices.C: Clotting proteins are made only by rocks.
choices.D: Inherited genes cannot affect body functions.

questions.correctAnswerText: A gene change can affect a protein involved in blood clotting.
questions.explanation: Genes can affect proteins, including proteins involved in blood clotting.
